document.source_title: Italy settles on Airbus tanker purchase in swing toward European equipment
document.author: Tom Kington
document.summary: Italy has finalized a €1.4 billion deal to purchase six Airbus A330 MRTT multi-role tanker transport aircraft, marking a significant shift from its previous reliance on Boeing tankers. This decision follows a 15-year history of operating Boeing aircraft and comes amid a broader trend among European militaries to seek greater commonality in equipment. The deal includes ten years of logistic support and reflects Italy's changing defense procurement strategy, particularly in light of previous plans to acquire KC-46 tankers.
key_takeaways: Italy to buy six Airbus A330 MRTT aircraft for €1.4 billion.
Shift from Boeing to Airbus for the Italian Air Force.
Deal includes ten years of logistic support.
Previous plans for KC-46 tankers were halted due to unforeseen needs.
European militaries are increasingly wary of U.S. military equipment.
Other European nations also operate the A330 MRTT through NATO pooling.
document.why_it_matters: This purchase signifies a strategic pivot for Italy towards European defense capabilities, reflecting broader trends in European military procurement and cooperation amidst geopolitical tensions.
